000.answer: No meaningful answer can be produced because there is no LLM, no retriever, and no knowledge base.
000.proof: Without core components, there is no chatbot behavior at all.

001.answer: A knowledge base by itself just stores information. It does not answer questions on its own.
001.proof: Documents sitting in storage are not the same as a chatbot understanding or answering from them.

010.answer: A retriever without a knowledge base has nothing to search, and without an LLM it cannot explain results.
010.proof: Search logic alone is not enough. It needs content to search and usually an LLM to synthesize an answer.

011.answer: The system can locate relevant internal passages, but without an LLM it cannot turn them into a natural language answer.
011.proof: Retrieval and generation are different jobs.

100.answer: I can generate a fluent response based on general language ability, but I do not have access to your proprietary internal manual.
100.proof: The LLM is not automatically trained on your internal documents.

101.answer: Even if a knowledge base exists, the LLM still cannot use it unless something retrieves the relevant information and passes it in.
101.proof: An LLM does not magically read a private document store just because the store exists.

110.answer: The LLM and retriever are present, but without a knowledge base there is no proprietary source to ground the answer in.
110.proof: A retriever needs a source of truth to search.

111.answer: Using retrieved passages from the internal knowledge base, I can generate a grounded answer. This is a RAG system: the LLM writes the answer, but the retriever supplies the relevant proprietary content.
111.proof: This shows the core idea of RAG: the LLM is not trained on the internal manual; it uses retrieved content at question time.
